steps.title: Identify what the passage says about surfactants
steps.content:
The study found that microplastic concentration alone cannot explain reduced wave activity. Chen and colleagues then found that surfactants — chemicals often used to manufacture plastics and released into the water from microplastics — have "a much stronger wave-reducing effect" than microplastics alone. The correct answer must reflect this specific finding.
  • "They have a much stronger effect on wave activity than microplastics alone do.": Directly restates the study's core finding about surfactants. ✓
  • "They are mainly composed of water.": Not mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • "They are helpful for removing microplastics from the ocean.": Not supported by the passage; the study is about wave reduction, not removal. ✗
  • "They can be used to contain microplastics within certain areas of the ocean.": Not mentioned in the passage. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only one choice matches the study's stated finding about surfactants and wave activity. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
